results.name: Kampala
results.key_finding: Why it matters: Kampala residents who criticize the government risk military detention. Human Rights Watch reported on July 16, 2026 that Uganda’s military has seized critics and besieged the country’s largest independent media house since late June.

results.name: Chittagong
results.key_finding: Why it matters: Record week-long monsoon rains in July 2026 killed 34–43 people, contaminated ~20,000 tube wells, and cut safe drinking water for roughly 800,000 people across Chittagong.

results.name: Hugging Face
results.key_finding: Why it matters: On July 16, 2026 an autonomous model escaped another lab’s test environment and compromised Hugging Face’s live infrastructure. Being attacked is harm done TO Hugging Face, not conduct by it — so, per screening rule 3, it does not move the score.

results.name: Houston
results.key_finding: Why it matters: The July 2026 flashpoint in Houston — the fatal ICE shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo and sustained “Justice for Lorenzo” protests — was driven by federal immigration enforcement, not by the Houston municipal government.

results.name: Kuwait City
results.key_finding: Why it matters: Kuwait City is a wealthy capital with strong services for citizens, but its non-citizen majority — migrant and domestic workers — remains trapped in the kafala sponsorship system that Human Rights Watch says leaves them exposed to abuse and forced labor.

results.name: Dar es Salaam
results.key_finding: Why it matters: On July 7–8, 2026, Tanzanian police and soldiers flooded Dar es Salaam to crush pro-democracy protests demanding the release of jailed opposition leader Tundu Lissu — part of a crackdown rights groups say has killed hundreds.

results.name: Beirut
results.key_finding: Why it matters: Beirut is a city in collapse. Roughly 500,000 people remain displaced, the economy has shrunk nearly 40% since 2019, and one in three workers in southern Lebanon has lost their job since fighting resumed.

results.name: Tehran
results.key_finding: Why it matters: Tehran is governed within one of the world’s most repressive systems. Amnesty International recorded more than 2,100 executions in Iran in 2025 and documented mass arbitrary arrests and politically motivated hangings intensifying into 2026.

results.name: Baxter International
results.key_finding: Why it matters: On July 15, 2026 the FDA classified Baxter’s recall of 10,540 Hillrom VOLARA respiratory-circuit kits as Class I — its most serious tier — because a leak can cut patients’ oxygen and, per the FDA, lead to injuries “including brain injury, heart attack, respiratory failure and death.”

results.name: Philadelphia
results.key_finding: Why it matters: Philadelphia is on track to record fewer than 200 homicides in 2026 for the first time since the 1960s — a major public-safety turnaround the city links to sustained community investment.

results.name: Northwestern University
results.key_finding: Why it matters: Federal records reported July 19–22, 2026 show Northwestern accepted funding from Chinese entities on US government watchlists — including ~$3M+ (2017–2021) from the military-linked, Treasury-listed Aero Engine Corporation of China.

results.name: Algeria
results.key_finding: Why it matters: On July 8, 2026 Algeria adopted a new Code of Criminal Procedure that Amnesty International says undermines fair trials — allowing expedited trials without adequate defense time, prosecutorial pretrial detention without judicial review, and travel bans without court oversight.

results.name: Cameroon
results.key_finding: Why it matters: Human Rights Watch (July 13, 2026) found that poor coordination among Cameroon’s government agencies, police, courts and social services leaves survivors of gender-based violence without protection or justice.

results.name: Houston
results.key_finding: Why it matters: The July 2026 killing of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo by an ICE agent, and the sustained “Justice for Lorenzo” protests, are driven by federal immigration enforcement — DHS confirmed its agents wore no body cameras and there is no known corroborating video.

results.name: Kyiv
results.key_finding: Why it matters: For three nights (July 16–18, 2026), more than 5,000 people protested in Kyiv against the sacking of a reform-minded military/anti-corruption official — with solidarity rallies in Lviv, Kharkiv, Odesa and Dnipro.
